heading: Use Match Expressions to Limit Trigger Invocations
body:
You can limit the number of Trigger invocations by specifying a [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) expression in the **Match
Expression** field. Atlas evaluates the match expression against the
change event document and invokes the Trigger only if the expression evaluates
to true for the given change event.

The match expression is a JSON document that specifies the query conditions
using the [MongoDB read query syntax](manual/reference/mql/query-operators).

We recommend only using match expressions when the volume of Trigger events
measurably becomes a performance issue. Until then, receive all events and
handle them individually in the Trigger Function code.

The exact shape of the change event document depends on the event that caused
the Trigger to fire. For details, see the reference for each event type:

- [insert](manual/reference/change-events/insert)

- [update](manual/reference/change-events/update)

- [replace](manual/reference/change-events/replace)

- [delete](manual/reference/change-events/delete)

- [create](manual/reference/change-events/create)

- [modify](manual/reference/change-events/modify)

- [rename](manual/reference/change-events/rename)

- [drop](manual/reference/change-events/drop)

- [shardCollection](manual/reference/change-events/shardCollection)

- [reshardCollection](manual/reference/change-events/reshardCollection)

- [refineCollectionShardKey](manual/reference/change-events/refineCollectionShardKey)

- [dropDatabase](manual/reference/change-events/dropDatabase)

The following match expression allows the Trigger to fire
only if the change event object specifies that the `status` field in
a document changed.

`updateDescription` is a field of the [update Event object](manual/reference/change-events/update).

```javascript
{
  "updateDescription.updatedFields.status": {
    "$exists": true
  }
}
```

The following match expression allows the Trigger to fire only when a
document's `needsTriggerResponse` field is `true`. The `fullDocument`
field of the [insert](manual/reference/change-events/insert),
[update](manual/reference/change-events/update), and [replace](manual/reference/change-events/replace) events represents a document after the
given operation. To receive the `fullDocument` field, you must enable
**Full Document** in your Trigger configuration.

```javascript
{
  "fullDocument.needsTriggerResponse": true
}
```
